channel.title: Evaluations on Esplanade Psychological Services
channel.description: Recent content in Evaluations on Esplanade Psychological Services

item.title: Neuropsychological Evaluation
item.description: A neuropsychological evaluation is a comprehensive assessment of how the brain is functioning across a range of skills. It helps clarify diagnoses, identify strengths and areas of vulnerability, and guide practical next steps at home, in school, and in treatment. Common referral questions Is ADHD, anxiety, depression, autism, or a learning disorder contributing to current difficulties? Why is there a gap between potential and performance? What accommodations (IEP/504, standardized testing) may be appropriate?

item.title: Psychoeducational Evaluation
item.description: A psychoeducational evaluation focuses on academic skills and learning processes. It helps clarify a student’s learning profile and identifies targeted supports to improve progress in school. When this may be helpful Reading, writing, spelling, or math concerns Suspected learning disability (including dyslexia) Planning for school supports (IEP/504) Clarifying why school performance isn’t matching effort or potential What families receive Review of records (report cards, prior evaluations, IEP/504) Interview and background history Standardized academic testing Clear written report with practical recommendations If you’re unsure whether a psychoeducational or full neuropsychological evaluation is the best fit, we can help you decide during an initial consultation.

item.title: ADHD Testing
item.description: ADHD symptoms can overlap with anxiety, depression, learning differences, sleep issues, and medical factors. A comprehensive evaluation helps clarify what is driving attention and executive-functioning challenges and what supports are most likely to help. What we look at Attention, working memory, and processing speed Executive functioning (planning, organization, inhibition, flexibility) Learning and academic skills (when relevant) Emotional functioning and stress/anxiety impacts Developmental, medical, and school history Outcomes Families and providers often use results to guide:

item.title: Autism Evaluation
item.description: An autism evaluation can help clarify diagnosis and identify individualized supports across home, school, and therapy settings. When families seek an autism evaluation Social communication differences Sensory sensitivities or rigidity/flexibility concerns Executive functioning challenges affecting daily life Emotional or behavioral symptoms impacting functioning Planning for school supports and accommodations What families receive Comprehensive history and record review Standardized measures selected for the referral questions Feedback session and collaborative discussion Integrated report with recommendations and next steps Start here: Contact / Request appointment.

item.title: Learning Disability / Dyslexia Evaluation
item.description: Learning differences can look like slow reading, difficulty with spelling/writing, math challenges, or inconsistent performance despite effort. An evaluation helps clarify underlying learning processes and guides targeted supports. Common reasons for referral Suspected dyslexia or other reading disorders Writing/spelling difficulties Math difficulties School progress not matching effort or potential Planning for tutoring/intervention and school accommodations What the evaluation can support Evidence-based intervention planning IEP/504 documentation and recommendations Standardized testing accommodations when appropriate To begin: Request an evaluation.

item.title: Executive Functioning Evaluation & Support
item.description: Executive functioning includes planning, organization, initiating tasks, time management, working memory, and flexible thinking. These skills can impact school, daily routines, and emotional well-being. We help clarify Whether executive functioning challenges are related to ADHD, anxiety, learning differences, autism, or other factors Which specific EF skills are most affected What strategies and supports are most likely to help Support options EF-focused therapy and skill-building Parent coaching and consultation School collaboration and recommendations Start here: Contact / Request appointment.

item.title: College & Standardized Testing Accommodations
item.description: Students may seek updated testing when transitioning to high school, college, or graduate programs, or when requesting accommodations for standardized exams. Common goals Clarify current learning and attention profile Provide clear recommendations for supports Document a history of need and current functioning If you have questions about timing or what documentation may be required, we can discuss this during an initial consultation. Start here: Contact / Request appointment.

item.title: School Consultation / IEP & 504 Support
item.description: When requested, we can consult with schools and treatment teams to help translate evaluation results into actionable supports. Examples of support Consultation with parents and schools about recommendations Participation in school meetings (e.g., IEP/504 planning) for an additional fee Guidance on accommodations and intervention planning If you’re a school professional or provider, see For Providers & Schools. To get started: Contact / Request appointment.
